FR-001.title: Writer ships inside the .vsix, copied at build time from the spec-kit ext source (single source of truth)

FR-002.title: Stock preamble references the bundled writer by quoted absolute extension path, never the workspace path

FR-003.title: Self-contradictory stock-mode prose corrected to the new mechanism

FR-004.title: Stock specify captures intent, expectations, context, per-requirement titles

FR-005.title: Stock plan captures approach/decisions/step summary; stock implement journals tasks, checks, coverage tests

FR-006.title: Capture stays best-effort (no python3 = silent skip); companion slim preambles unchanged

FR-007.title: Preamble builders unit-tested: bundled path, quoting, ICE instructions per step

FR-008.title: Sandbox eval: companion-free stock run with forced GUI preamble asserts context outcomes

FR-009.title: capture-and-timing doc stock-mode section + root changelog updated in-change

context: area: src/ai-providers/promptPreamble.ts + promptBuilder.ts (stock vs slim branches) + packaging
constraint: extension-isolation — prompt text built by shipped code is the sanctioned surface; never depend on workspace .specify
constraint: single source of truth speckit-extension/scripts/write-context.py; companion slim preamble byte-identical
intent: Make stock-mode GUI dispatches capture reliably (bundled writer) and richly (ICE/decisions/checks/coverage) so the Activity panel works without the spec-kit extension
expectations: no Activity panel changes — it already renders these fields
companion-mode capture protocol unchanged (command bodies keep owning it)
eval not wired into jest — committed re-runnable script
approach: Ship write-context.py inside the .vsix via a .vscodeignore negation (no copy step — the dev host and the package resolve the same relative path), thread a quoted bundled-writer path from promptBuilder into the pure preamble functions, and add a compact per-step capture block to the stock preamble reusing the writer's existing flags. Prove it with a committed sandbox eval that forces the exact preamble on a headless AI in a companion-free workspace.

decisions.decision: ship the script by un-ignoring it in .vscodeignore
decisions.why: stdlib-only single file; byte-identical by construction; dev host resolves the same relative path
decisions.rejected: build-time copy (wiring + drift risk) or a committed duplicate

decisions.decision: path lookup in promptBuilder via vscode.extensions, path parameter into pure promptPreamble
decisions.why: keeps the preamble module vscode-free and unit-testable; builder is the existing impure seam
decisions.rejected: threading ExtensionContext or module-level mutable state

decisions.decision: bundled path always in stock mode, even with the companion ext installed
decisions.why: one code path, trivially assertable, same file by construction

decisions.decision: eval = real headless AI on the forced preamble in a temp sandbox, outside jest
decisions.why: only honest test of instruction-following; text snapshots cannot catch a model misreading
decisions.rejected: preamble snapshot tests alone, or a full spec-kit-init sandbox (tests the wrong layer)

plan.summary: Plan + research + data-model + preamble contract; no schema changes, one manifest negation, one threaded parameter, one committed eval
plan.key_finding: write-context.py is stdlib-only and self-contained, so a .vscodeignore negation ships it byte-identical with zero build wiring
